methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Terrestrial Energy operates in the nuclear energy sector at the public stage, having raised $280M to date. The valuation landscape comprises 11 peers across nuclear energy: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private entities including Oklo, Nano Nuclear Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

Peer-median valuation analysis is not applicable here. The nuclear energy peer set, while containing 8 same-sector companies, lacks sufficient depth within narrow stage and tier filters—only 2 same-stage peers were identified, falling short of the minimum 3 datapoints required for statistical reliability. Adjacent-stage and similar-valuation-tier peers numbered zero, further constraining conventional multiples-based triangulation. A sector-wide median would introduce unacceptable heterogeneity across development stages and commercial maturity.

Given these constraints, traditional comparable-company valuation cannot be reliably anchored to market data. Any valuation assessment would require alternative approaches—transaction multiples (if M&A precedents exist), discounted cash flow analysis incorporating development timelines and regulatory approval risk, or staged financing rounds as market-implied valuation checkpoints. The $280M cumulative raise provides a floor, though it reflects capital deployed rather than enterprise value. Key uncertainties include regulatory pathway duration, capital intensity to commercialization, and the nascent public market's valuation of pre-revenue nuclear technologies.
